description: 84-line project kickoff planning session with 4 speakers scheduling 8 events for a product launch spanning the next 5 weeks. Tests multi-week date tracking and distinguishing scheduled meetings from planning discussion.

events.title: Horizon Weekly Sync
events.reasoning: Alice establishes a recurring Tuesday 10 AM Horizon sync starting February 24th, 1 hour, all four team members.
events.assumptions: Only the first occurrence is created; recurrence noted

events.title: Demo Prep Session
events.reasoning: Alice schedules a demo prep session for Friday February 27th at 3 PM for 1 hour with all four team members.

events.title: Technical Review
events.reasoning: Dave and Alice agree on a technical review for March 2nd at 2 PM for 2 hours, with Bob and Carol also attending.

events.title: Internal Stakeholder Demo
events.reasoning: Alice plans a 2-hour internal demo on Monday March 9th at 2 PM for the full team plus marketing and exec stakeholders.

events.title: Customer Success Training
events.reasoning: Alice and Bob plan a 2-hour customer success training session on Monday March 16th at 1 PM with the CS team.

events.title: Launch Readiness Review
events.reasoning: Alice schedules a 90-minute launch readiness review for Wednesday March 18th at 9 AM with the full team and QA lead.

events.title: Post-Launch Retrospective
events.reasoning: Alice schedules a post-launch retrospective for Thursday March 26th at 2 PM for 90 minutes, one week after launch.

mock_llm_response.summary: Found 7 events: Horizon weekly sync (Feb 24), demo prep (Feb 27), technical review (Mar 2), internal stakeholder demo (Mar 9), customer success training (Mar 16), launch readiness review (Mar 18), and post-launch retrospective (Mar 26). The launch date of March 19th and code freeze of March 14th are planning markers, not calendar meetings.

notes: Reference datetime is Friday 2026-02-20. Events span 5 weeks. The journalist press call (March 19th 8 AM) was mentioned briefly and is intentionally omitted from expected_events due to ambiguity in scope; relaxed tolerance accommodates this. Code freeze (March 14th) is a deadline marker, not a meeting event.
